notes: Assertions check the discipline the skill teaches that a no-skill baseline cannot know: edit the khenrix-utils REPO (not the installed plugin copy), split changes into repo-edits (applied with confirmation) vs live-config (recommend-only, never auto-applied), preserve each skill's purpose, and write a dated report under docs/upgrades/. Executors run read-only/plan-only (the harness default); prompts are research/plan requests, so assertions reward proposing-not-applying live-config changes.

evals.name: modernize-claude-setup
evals.prompt: Modernize how this machine uses Claude Code — research the latest changes and best practices and improve my setup.
assertions: Edits the khenrix-utils repo (the directory with capabilities.toml / .git), NOT the installed plugin copy, and runs make khenrix-refresh after
Separates changes into two buckets: repo edits applied with confirmation, vs live-config tuning that is only recommended (never auto-applied)
Preserves each skill's purpose — improves wording/structure/triggering/model usage, not the behaviour a skill delivers
Plans to write a dated report under docs/upgrades/ with findings and copy-paste live-config commands

evals.name: model-change-is-recommendation-only
evals.prompt: Find the best current Claude model for my setup and just switch me over to it.
assertions: Treats the live model change as a recommendation with the exact command, rather than silently switching the live config itself
Researches the current recommended model before answering rather than guessing
Distinguishes a repo change (e.g. model guidance in the skills / house-style, applied with confirmation) from the live-config switch (report-only)
